slides.name: Title Slide
slides.notes: First impression. Make it memorable. Tagline should pass the 'mom test' — anyone should understand what you do.

slides.name: Problem
slides.notes: Make the investor FEEL the problem. Use a story or data point. Avoid abstract problems — be specific about who hurts and how much.

slides.name: Solution
slides.notes: Show, don't tell. If you can demo, demo. 3-step explanation max. The 'aha moment' should be obvious.

slides.name: Market Size
slides.notes: Investors care about SAM/SOM more than TAM. Show bottom-up math, not top-down 'if we get 1% of a $100B market'. Reference credible sources.

slides.name: Business Model
slides.notes: Show you understand the money. LTV:CAC ratio >3:1 is healthy. Payback period <12 months is ideal for SaaS.

slides.name: Traction
slides.notes: This is the most important slide for early-stage. Show momentum. Even pre-revenue traction counts: waitlist, LOIs, pilots, partnerships.

slides.name: Competition
slides.notes: Never say 'no competition'. Use a 2x2 matrix with axes that make you look good (choose axes where you're in the top-right). Be honest about strengths of competitors.

slides.name: Go-to-Market
slides.notes: Show you know HOW to reach customers, not just that they exist. Specificity > ambition. 'We'll acquire first 100 users through X' beats 'we'll do digital marketing'.

slides.name: Team
content: Why THIS team for THIS problem
slides.notes: Founder-market fit is critical. Show domain expertise, prior exits, or unique insights. If team is thin, show you know who to hire next.

slides.name: Financials
slides.notes: Be realistic. Investors will stress-test your assumptions. Show you understand the levers: # customers × ARPU × retention. Include bear/base/bull cases if possible.

slides.name: The Ask
slides.notes: Be specific. '€200K to achieve X, Y, Z milestones in 12 months' is better than '€200K for growth'. Show the bridge to next round or profitability.

slides.name: Appendix
slides.notes: Have these ready for Q&A but don't present them unless asked.
